Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. OpenBSD on SGI: A Rollercoaster Story 66 upvotes, 3 comments
  2. Show HN: Jido 2.0, Elixir Agent Framework 95 upvotes, 16 comments
  3. Google Workspace CLI 810 upvotes, 263 comments
  4. Building a new Flash 653 upvotes, 212 comments
  5. Wikipedia in read-only mode following mass admin account compromise 182 upvotes, 55 comments
  6. Nvidia PersonaPlex 7B on Apple Silicon: Full-Duplex Speech-to-Speech in Swift 291 upvotes, 95 comments
  7. Google Safe Browsing missed 84% of confirmed phishing sites 161 upvotes, 49 comments
  8. Jails for NetBSD – Kernel Enforced Isolation and Native Resource Control 79 upvotes, 21 comments
  9. Arabic document from 17th-cent. rubbish heap confirms semi-legendary Nubian king 115 upvotes, 36 comments
  10. Something is afoot in the land of Qwen 755 upvotes, 325 comments
  11. Poor Man's Polaroid 133 upvotes, 47 comments
  12. Earth Garden: Field Recordings Around the World 40 upvotes, 10 comments
  13. Smalltalk's Browser: Unbeatable, yet Not Enough 109 upvotes, 42 comments
  14. World-first gigabit laser link between aircraft and geostationary satellite 102 upvotes, 39 comments
  15. Greg Kroah-Hartman Stretches Support Periods for Key Linux LTS Kernels 16 upvotes, 3 comments
  16. Fast-Servers 47 upvotes, 19 comments
  17. Good software knows when to stop 126 upvotes, 68 comments
  18. Intelligence is a commodity. Context is the real AI Moat 65 upvotes, 32 comments
  19. The L in "LLM" Stands for Lying 536 upvotes, 353 comments
  20. A GitHub Issue Title Compromised 4k Developer Machines 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  21. No right to relicense this project 403 upvotes, 266 comments
  22. Judge orders government to begin refunding more than $130B in tariffs 588 upvotes, 447 comments
  23. The IRIX 6.5.7M (sgi) source code 28 upvotes, 14 comments
  24. Datasets for Reconstructing Visual Perception from Brain Data 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  25. The Man Who Broke into Jail 39 upvotes, 25 comments
  26. AMD will bring its “Ryzen AI” processors to standard desktop PCs for first time 178 upvotes, 154 comments
  27. Billy bookshelves as a retro motherboard "rack" 49 upvotes, 38 comments
  28. Relicensing with AI-Assisted Rewrite 304 upvotes, 310 comments
  29. MacBook Neo 1885 upvotes, 2181 comments
  30. The Government Uses Targeted Advertising to Track Your Location 4 upvotes, 1 comments

Last generated 1:30am, 6 March 2026. Source here.
